T0306.title: Onboard A Card
T0306.definition: Card onboarding refers to the act of an individual providing their own bank card to underground money-laundering networks for receiving and moving illicit funds.
T0306.description: To evade fund tracing, criminal networks recruit card mules who supply their bank cards as collection and transfer tools. Lured by commissions, participants knowingly or recklessly hand over their cards, U-shields, and account access for receiving fraud proceeds. Once implicated, card providers face account freezes, credit blacklisting, and potential criminal liability.
T0306.usageExample: A new broker claims you just need to onboard a card and run a transaction to get an 88 USDT red packet—sounds completely shady.

references.title: Exposing Fraud-Laundering Models: Order-Posting Recharge Laundering and Merchant Laundering Spread Rapidly
The Current Card Vendor Ecosystem Under the Card-Crackdown Campaign
